expected_failure_modes: Hovering indefinitely at high altitude to avoid penalties – rewarded too little by -y.
Descending too fast and crashing – penalised by velocity/tilt terms, but may still occur if terminal success bonus is insufficient relative to dense shaping.
Oscillating sideways near ground – lateral movement penalised, but side engine usage may be high.
Bouncing after first contact – not captured by events unless second contact triggers safe_landing later; could be handled by requiring both contacts stable.
Uncentered touchdown – only penalised by -abs(x) in dense guidance, so agent may still land off‑center if other terms dominate.
risk_notes: Coefficients in r_progress_guidance were chosen heuristically; they may need scaling depending on the actual environment dynamics.
The near-ground condition y < 0.5 assumes the altitude scale such that 0.5 is a reasonable ‘low altitude’ threshold; if ground is not at y=0, or if the coordinate system differs, this will fail.

Crash event may trigger even if safe_landing could have triggered a moment later; the condition '(left_contact OR right_contact)' at high speed should precede safe_landing conditions, preventing double reward. Runtime must ensure events are evaluated once at terminal step.
Terminal events rely on the episode terminating on ground contact; if the simulation continues after contact, these events could fire multiple times.
Action cost uses abs(side_engine) to penalise any lateral thrust; if the environment normalises side_engine differently, the coefficient may need adjustment.
